text:
Accurate traffic flow prediction is a cornerstone of Intelligent Transportation Systems with vast practical implications. The primary difficulty lies in modeling the intricate and evolving spatial-temporal dependencies inherent in urban traffic data. Small-scale or static approaches often fail to capture the full picture. While Spatial-Temporal Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have shown promise, they suffer from three critical shortcomings: they typically rely on static spatial modeling that misses dynamic shifts in traffic; they focus on localized neighborhoods, failing to account for long-range dependencies between distant but functionally similar areas; and they overlook the inherent time delays in how traffic conditions propagate from one location to another.

To overcome these hurdles, we introduce PDFormer, a Propagation Delay-aware dynamic long-range Transformer. Our model features a specialized spatial self-attention module designed to track dynamic spatial relationships as they change over time. By incorporating two unique graph masking matrices, PDFormer can simultaneously emphasize short-range physical proximity and long-range semantic similarities. Crucially, we propose a traffic delay-aware feature transformation module that enables the model to explicitly account for the time lag in information propagation across the network. Comprehensive testing on six real-world datasets demonstrates that PDFormer achieves state-of-the-art accuracy while maintaining high computational efficiency. Furthermore, we provide visualizations of the learned attention maps, offering clear interpretability into how the model perceives complex traffic patterns.
